notes: Antigua and Barbuda should score English as fully compatible while also crediting Antigua and Barbuda Creole English because it exists in the picker catalog and is part of everyday speech.

evidence.title: Britannica: Antigua and Barbuda
evidence.note: Britannica identifies English as the official language of Antigua and Barbuda.

evidence.title: Visit Antigua & Barbuda
evidence.note: The official tourism authority serves visitors in English, supporting full travel usability in English.

notes: The United Kingdom should score English as fully compatible, while also recognizing Welsh and Scottish Gaelic as meaningful regional matches.

evidence.title: VisitBritain
evidence.note: Britain's official tourism site serves international visitors in English, supporting full traveler usability in English.

evidence.title: Britannica: United Kingdom
evidence.note: Britannica describes English as dominant across the United Kingdom and notes Welsh and Scottish Gaelic in their regional contexts.

notes: French Guiana should score French as fully compatible, with Guianese Creole French also credited because it exists in the picker catalog and reflects local everyday speech.

evidence.title: Britannica: French Guiana
evidence.note: Britannica identifies French as the official language of French Guiana.

evidence.title: Guyane Amazonie
evidence.note: French Guiana's official tourism site operates primarily in French, supporting French as the main traveler language.

notes: Iraq should score Arabic and Central Kurdish as official practical matches, with broader Kurdish also credited and English given moderate tourism/business usefulness.

evidence.title: Britannica: Iraq summary
evidence.note: Britannica identifies Arabic and Kurdish as the official languages of Iraq.

evidence.title: Britannica: Iraq languages
evidence.note: Britannica explains that Arabic is the main official language while Kurdish is official in the autonomous Kurdish region, with multilingualism common across the country.

notes: Papua New Guinea should credit English, Tok Pisin, and Hiri Motu because all three function in public life and Tok Pisin is especially useful in everyday communication.

evidence.title: Papua New Guinea Travel: Learn the Language
evidence.note: Papua New Guinea's official tourism guidance says English is practical for tourists and encourages learning Tok Pisin for local interaction.

evidence.title: Papua New Guinea Travel: Languages spoken in Papua New Guinea
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance explains the roles of English, Tok Pisin, and Hiri Motu in Papua New Guinea.

notes: Serbia should score Serbian as the primary local language while giving strong English credit in travel contexts because official tourism infrastructure serves visitors in English.

evidence.title: Britannica: Serbia
evidence.note: Britannica identifies Serbian as the principal language of Serbia.

evidence.title: National Tourism Organisation of Serbia
evidence.note: Serbia's official tourism organization maintains an English-facing visitor site, supporting strong tourism usability in English.

notes: Somalia should score Somali and Arabic as official core languages, with smaller English credit for international-facing contexts.

evidence.title: Britannica: Somalia
evidence.note: Britannica identifies Somali and Arabic as the official languages of Somalia.

evidence.title: Britannica: Semitic languages
evidence.note: Britannica lists Arabic as one of Somalia's official languages.

notes: Taiwan should score Mandarin Chinese as the main national language, while also crediting Taiwanese/Min Nan, Hakka, and strong English usability in traveler contexts.

evidence.title: Taiwan Tourism Administration
evidence.note: Taiwan's official tourism administration maintains a substantial English-language site for foreign visitors, supporting real tourism usability in English.

evidence.title: Britannica: Taiwan
evidence.note: Britannica describes Mandarin Chinese as the standard language in Taiwan while also discussing Taiwanese and Hakka usage.

notes: The United States Minor Outlying Islands are administered in English. Some islands are uninhabited or restricted, but English is still the practical administrative language tied to the territory.

evidence.title: Britannica: United States Minor Outlying Islands
evidence.note: Britannica describes the territory's special administrative status under the United States.

evidence.title: U.S. Department of the Interior: Insular Areas
evidence.note: U.S. government information for the territory is administered in English.

notes: Kosovo should score Albanian as the main everyday language, with Serbian also counting directly and English given strong visitor usability.

evidence.title: Britannica: Kosovo
evidence.note: Britannica identifies Albanian and Serbian as the principal official languages used in Kosovo.

evidence.title: Kosovo tourism site
evidence.note: Kosovo's tourism-facing infrastructure is delivered in English for international visitors, supporting strong tourism usability in English.
